blocks.content: Maya turned back to the window, which Nathan read correctly as a signal and not a rejection — the difference being one of posture, of which he had become, in recent years, a more careful student.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Can I ask you something directly?

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Are you okay? Not tonight-okay. Actually.

She was quiet. Not the quiet of someone deciding whether to answer — the quiet of someone who had been asked this question in a number of ways recently and was deciding how much honesty was appropriate for this particular asker.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: That's a bigger question than this conversation has room for.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Fair. I'll put it differently. Is there anything tonight that would make it better?
She looked at him. Something in his expression was open in a way she hadn't expected — not angling for anything, not performing sensitivity. Just genuinely asking.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: All of it. This kind of thing. I don't get enough of it.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: Then let's go back to the table. And I'll stop strategically placing myself at windows.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You can place yourself where you want. Just —
And he did. He pushed off the windowsill and held out his hand — not romantically, just the gesture of someone indicating direction.
Maya walked back toward the others. He let out a slow breath at the window. Then he followed.
